tip_description: Seatbelts reduce fatal injuries in crashes by up to 50%.
Maintain speed limits to avoid loss of control.
Keep a 3-second gap from the vehicle ahead.
Always check mirrors and blind spots before changing lanes.
Never use mobile phones while driving.
Drive slowly in areas with poor road conditions.
Inform authorities if you see a non-functioning traffic light.
Do not attempt to cross flooded streets.
In poorly lit roads, always use headlights.
Check road alerts before travel.
Do not engage with reckless drivers, maintain distance.
Never drive against the flow of traffic.
Note vehicle number and inform traffic police.
Avoid carrying excess passengers or cargo.
Motorcyclists should always wear helmets for safety.
Wet roads are slippery, reduce speed and increase distance.
In fog, turn on fog lights and keep speed low.
Check tire pressure in extreme heat.
Avoid parking under trees or poles during windstorms.
First aid, torch, and fire extinguisher should always be in car.
Check brakes, tyres, and engine regularly to prevent breakdowns.
Keep helplines like 1122, Police 15 saved on your phone.
